div.p: Point-in-time capture of state machine state

p.strong: Snapshot File Layout (JSON with binary data)

div.p: Version Offset: 0x04 Size: 4 bytes Format version = 1
MetadataLen Offset: 0x08 Size: 4 bytes Length of JSON metadata
Metadata (JSON) Offset: 0x0C Size: M bytes json { "lastIncludedIndex": 10000, "lastIncludedTerm": 5 }
DataLen Offset: 0x0C+M Size: 4 bytes Length of snapshot data
Data (State Machine) Offset: 0x10+M Size: D bytes Application-specific serialized state
Checksum Offset: 0x10+M+D Size: 4 bytes CRC32 of all bytes

ul.li: lastIncludedIndex: Index of last log entry in snapshot
lastIncludedTerm: Term of entry at lastIncludedIndex Both fields are CRITICAL for:

div.p: Step 2: fsync temp file Ensures data reaches disk
Step 4: fsync directory Ensures rename is durable

ol.li:
Atomicity: Snapshot is persisted BEFORE log truncation
Crash recovery: old log OR new snapshot exists
Never partial/corrupt state

Consistency: lastIncludedIndex matches actual coverage
Snapshot represents entries 1..lastIncludedIndex
Log contains entries lastIncludedIndex+1..N

Recoverability: State machine can restore from Data field
Application implements RestoreSnapshot([]byte) error
Session state included for deduplication continuity
